title: Rathschläge für eine Orientreise
summary: The checked-in CME00014 XML represents the University of Michigan CME item keyed from Carl Horstmann's 'Rathschläge für eine Orientreise' in Englische Studien 8 (1885), pp. 277–284. Horstmann transcribed an anonymous, apparently acephalous Middle English prose guide for an affluent traveler and entourage from British Library Cotton MS Appendix VIII, ff. 108v–112v. The British Library dates this manuscript portion to the second half of the fifteenth century and identifies the full parchment codex as i + 112 leaves, but its current record exposes no digitized content. A complete 29,447,431-byte PDF of the 522-page Internet Archive scan of Johnson Reprint Corporation's 1965 reprint of the 1885 volume is cached locally; the article maps to PDF pages 287–294. The repository XML, U-M encoding, 1885 article, 1965 reprint, manuscript witness, and scan are modeled separately.
review_notes: The lineage's intellectual subject is the anonymous Middle English travel-advice fragment, not Horstmann's German title, the whole Englische Studien volume, or the adjacent Life of Our Lady in Cotton MS Appendix VIII.

Horstmann's source note gives Cotton Appendix VIII, f. 108 ff.; the current British Library record fixes the guide at ff. 108v–112v and dates that portion independently from the preceding Lydgate text.

The cached journal PDF covers the complete scanned reprint volume. Printed p. 277 begins with the end of the preceding article before Horstmann's contribution starts, and p. 284 begins the next contribution after it ends.

The British Library catalog's empty Digitised Content field and failed checks of plausible legacy/IIIF routes support only a dated negative public-access finding, not a claim that no institutional photography exists.
Rights in the medieval text, Horstmann's old article, the 1965 reprint object, Internet Archive scan, U-M encoding, and checked-in XML are separate layers.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00014
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME00014
entities.description: An electronic encoding of Horstmann's contribution. The XML presents one DIV1 typed as a treatise, preserves the German heading and two editorial headnotes, the Middle English prose, eight printed-page milestones, three manuscript-folio milestones, and nine footnotes.
notes: The compact source exposes no item-level statement identifying manual keying, OCR, correction level, or original electronic-publication date.
A revision note dated 16 June 2016 records structural validation, title proofing, page-break checking, and other markup review.
The current U-M route presented an automated-client challenge during review; the stable handle and public item route remain recorded without treating accessibility as a license statement.

entities.label: Horstmann's 1885 edition of the Cotton Appendix VIII travel guide
entities.description: The immediate print source for CME00014: Carl Horstmann's edition of the Middle English prose on Cotton Appendix VIII ff. 108v–112v, with a supplied German title, a note that the beginning appears to be missing, folio markers, nine short textual or lexical notes, and no separate introduction or apparatus.

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, 'Rathschläge für eine Orientreise,' Englische Studien 8 (Heilbronn: Verlag von Gebr. Henninger, 1885), 277–284.
bibliographic.title: Rathschläge für eine Orientreise

notes: The title is Horstmann's supplied German heading; the manuscript beginning is not available publicly for checking whether it carried an original title.
Printed p. 277 is shared with the preceding contribution, and p. 284 is shared with the following contribution.
Horstmann's marginal source markers show f. 109, f. 110, and f. 112 within the text; the current British Library component boundary provides the complete modern range ff. 108v–112v.

entities.label: Johnson Reprint Corporation's 1965 reprint of Englische Studien 8
entities.description: A complete reprint of the 1885 volume. Its preliminaries retain the original Heilbronn title page and add Johnson Reprint Corporation and Johnson Reprint Company Limited statements, 'First reprinting, 1965,' and printing by Anton Hain KG in West Germany.

bibliographic.citation: Englische Studien, vol. 8 (Heilbronn: Verlag von Gebr. Henninger, 1885; reprinted New York and London: Johnson Reprint Corporation / Johnson Reprint Company Limited, 1965).
bibliographic.title: Englische Studien
bibliographic.publisher: Johnson Reprint Corporation / Johnson Reprint Company Limited

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Appendix VIII
entities.description: A composite Middle English parchment codex containing John Lydgate's Life of Our Lady on ff. 2r–108r and the anonymous pilgrimage or travel guide cataloged here on ff. 108v–112v. The two portions have separate British Library date statements.

identifiers.scheme: British Library Archives and Manuscripts record

date_statements.label: production of the travel-guide portion
date_statements.value: second half of the fifteenth century
date_statements.notes: This is the holding institution's date for ff. 108v–112v, not a precise composition date for the abstract work.

date_statements.label: production of the preceding Life of Our Lady portion
date_statements.value: first half of the fifteenth century
date_statements.notes: This separate date belongs to ff. 2r–108r and is retained only to prevent transfer of the adjacent work's date to the guide.

holding.notes: The current record has no Digitised Content link and requires a letter of introduction to view the manuscript onsite.

physical_description.extent: i + 112 leaves; the cataloged work occupies ff. 108v–112v
physical_description.format: Composite codex; British Museum binding dated 1864
notes: The British Library describes the object as intact.
The early modern endleaf is foliated i; the manuscript leaves are ff. 1–112.
The catalog gives England as origin for the manuscript object.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of the 1965 Englische Studien 8 reprint
entities.description: A complete 522-page PDF scan of the Johnson reprint volume. It includes the reprint and original title pages, complete contents, printed pp. 1–511, and boundary blanks; Horstmann's article occupies PDF pages 287–294.
entities.reproduction_format: 522-page text PDF; 29,447,431 bytes; complete local direct-download copy
notes: Complete refers to the scanned 1965 physical reprint volume, not to a manuscript facsimile.
The article mapping preserves both shared boundary pages rather than cropping adjacent contributions.

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright
entities.description: Current federal guidance used only for the manifest's jurisdiction-limited assessment of the 1885 article; it is not part of the textual lineage.

assertion.notes: The IDs, collection path, stable UMDL handle, and represented article align; a separately downloadable current U-M source XML was not available for byte comparison.

subject.description: German heading and headnotes, Middle English prose, notes, printed pagination, and manuscript-folio milestones
Middle English transcription and its textual notes

object.description: The travel or pilgrimage guide, excluding the preceding Life of Our Lady

subject.description: The reprinted Horstmann article within the complete 1965 volume

scope.notes: The reprint preliminaries identify the object as the first 1965 reprinting; no textual revision of Horstmann's article was observed.

access.access_method: Read from the pinned CME submodule in this repository.
Open the stable UMDL handle in a browser; it redirects to the public U-M Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item.
notes: The stable handle resolved, but the current content endpoint returned an automated-client challenge during this review; this practical condition is not treated as a use license.

access.access_method: Open the item page or download the complete PDF directly.

local_copies.label: Complete Englische Studien volume 8 PDF

work_portion.label: Rathschläge für eine Orientreise
notes: Printed p. 277 begins with the end of the preceding contribution before this article starts; printed p. 284 ends this article and then begins the next contribution.
The CME00014 work-local path is hardlinked to the byte-identical complete-volume object already verified for CME00011; the exact source URL, byte count, and SHA-256 are identical.

access.access_method: Open the exact British Library Archives and Manuscripts record.
Request Cotton MS Appendix VIII through the collection item request form and consult it at the British Library in London; the item record states that a letter of introduction is required.
No public complete-codex viewer, PDF, or IIIF Presentation manifest was found; use the exact catalog record and Reference Services route to ask whether unlinked complete photography exists.
notes: The current catalog's Digitised Content field is empty.
A plausible BL Digirati endpoint based on the record MDARK returned access denied, and a plausible iiif.bl.uk manifest path returned not found.
This is a dated negative public search, not proof that the Library holds no internal, microfilm, or requestable photography.
The CME00014 cache path is a hardlink to a previously verified byte-identical repository cache object with the same exact URL, size, and SHA-256.

rights.component: Horstmann's 1885 article, including its transcription, German editorial matter, and notes
rights.basis: The article was published in 1885. U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A describes a maximum 95-year term for works already protected before 1978; that maximum has expired for this publication. This assessment is not a worldwide license statement.

rights.component: Internet Archive PDF scan derivative of the 1965 Johnson reprint
rights.jurisdiction: Item-specific provider statement and intended reuse jurisdiction
rights.basis: The PDF is publicly downloadable, but the item metadata inspected during review supplied no item-specific Creative Commons license or rights statement for the scan derivative. Public access is not treated as permission for redistribution.

rights.component: U-M electronic transcription, structure, and markup
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended reuse jurisdiction
rights.basis: No item-level rights text was verifiable through the challenged current endpoint during this review. The public stable handle and age of the source edition do not by themselves license the electronic markup.

rights.component: Checked-in XML serialization and any repository-specific modifications
rights.jurisdiction: Repository terms and intended reuse jurisdiction
rights.basis: The local file contains public-domain source text but no embedded license that independently covers its serialization, markup, or later modifications.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A guide occupying one short component at the end of a composite manuscript, apparently lacking its textual beginning
editorial_practices.representation: A continuous edition of ff. 108v–112v with a supplied German title, an explicit note that the beginning seems to be missing, selected folio milestones, and short footnotes
editorial_practices.description: Horstmann publishes the guide as a discrete contribution but does not silently supply the absent opening or treat the preceding Life of Our Lady as part of it.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Horstmann's contribution embedded between adjacent articles in a 511-page journal volume
editorial_practices.representation: One DIV1 typed as treatise, containing only Horstmann's heading, two headnotes, the prose transcription, notes, and page/folio milestones
editorial_practices.description: The encoding extracts the complete contribution from the journal container while retaining its printed page numbers 277–284; it does not encode the volume title page, contents, or neighboring articles.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Italic letters and letter groups embedded within words throughout Horstmann's Middle English transcription
editorial_practices.representation: HI elements with REND='italic' preserve the printed italic segments
editorial_practices.description: The typography is consistent with expanded manuscript abbreviations, but Horstmann provides no general method statement in this short article; the XML therefore preserves a print-level distinction rather than proving each manuscript abbreviation independently.
notes: Do not reinterpret every HI element as emphasis.

evidence.citation: General Corpus / CME source XML, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00014.xml.
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00014 through IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID; records the 16 June 2016 review; and encodes Horstmann's heading, source and incompleteness headnotes, 94 P elements, 8 PB elements, 3 folio MILESTONE elements, 9 NOTE elements, and the complete Middle English prose contribution.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, stable item CME00014.
evidence.locator: Stable item handle and current item route
evidence.summary: The stable UMDL identifier resolves toward the U-M CME collection. The current content route returned an automated-client challenge during review, so no item-level bibliographic or rights claim is based solely on inaccessible page text.

evidence.citation: Carl Horstmann, 'Rathschläge für eine Orientreise,' Englische Studien 8 (Heilbronn: Verlag von Gebr. Henninger, 1885), 277–284.
evidence.summary: Direct edition evidence for Horstmann's supplied title and byline, Cotton Appendix VIII f. 108 ff. source statement, apparent missing beginning, complete Middle English text, manuscript folio markers, nine notes, and article boundaries.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of the complete Internet Archive PDF for Englische Studien 8 and its article pages.
evidence.locator: PDF preliminaries pp. 1–6; article PDF pp. 287–294; closing PDF pp. 515–522
evidence.summary: Confirms the original 1885 Heilbronn title page, Johnson's first-reprinting statement dated 1965, complete volume contents, the article's shared first and last printed pages, final C. Horstmann byline, continuation through printed p. 511, and closing blank surface.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item englische-studien_1885_8, Englische Studien 1885: Vol. 8.
evidence.locator: Item metadata; complete PDF; article printed pp. 277–284
evidence.summary: Provides the complete publicly downloadable 29,447,431-byte PDF, 522 PDF pages, Internet Archive identifier and ARK, and item-level scan metadata. No item-specific open license was found in the reviewed metadata.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Appendix VIII, record 040-001103963.
evidence.locator: Title and scope; ff. 108v–112v; dates; physical characteristics; origin; access; publications; Digitised Content
evidence.summary: The holding institution identifies the pilgrimage-guide to the East at ff. 108v–112v, dates those leaves to the second half of the fifteenth century, distinguishes the first-half-fifteenth-century Lydgate portion on ff. 2r–108r, and gives English origin, parchment support, i + 112 leaves, approximately 230 × 170 mm dimensions, intact condition, 1864 binding, exact shelfmark, onsite conditions, and no Digitised Content link.

evidence.citation: Dated public-facsimile access checks for British Library Cotton MS Appendix VIII.
evidence.summary: The exact catalog had no Digitised Content URL; the MDARK-based Digirati route returned 403, the tested iiif.bl.uk manifest route returned 404, and no complete public facsimile was found through exact shelfmark, record-ID, or ARK searches. This negative finding is limited to public routes checked on 11 July 2026.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Circular 15A: Duration of Copyright.
evidence.locator: p. 2, works already under statutory protection before 1978; p. 3, restoration caveat
evidence.summary: Current official guidance states the maximum 95-year U.S. term for works already protected before 1978 and explains the separate restoration issue for certain foreign works; it supports only the manifest's conservative United States assessment of the 1885 publication.

open_questions.question: Can a complete color facsimile of the physical Cotton MS Appendix VIII object be obtained and retained locally?
next_steps: Ask British Library Reference Services whether unlinked complete color photography, legacy microfilm, or an internal digital surrogate exists for Cotton MS Appendix VIII.

If ordering new photography, request the complete physical object: binding and spine where offered, boards or covers, pastedowns, the early modern endleaf, all manuscript leaves ff. 1–112 including blanks and the adjacent Life of Our Lady, and any terminal or inserted surfaces.
Map CME00014 separately to ff. 108v–112v and record exact image or canvas locators plus first/end deep links in work_portion.
Confirm written retention and reuse terms before caching any repository-supplied files or publishing derivatives.
notes: No manuscript image was cached because no public image deliverable was verified.

open_questions.question: What keying method, correction history, selection rationale, and item-level rights statement apply to the U-M CME00014 encoding?
next_steps: Recheck the current U-M item page when automated-client access is available or contact the Digital Collections team for item-level production metadata.
Request any downloadable source package and explicit terms covering the electronic transcription and markup.
Compare representative XML readings against Horstmann's scanned article without assuming OCR or manual keying.

open_questions.question: Does every italic segment in Horstmann's transcription represent an expanded manuscript abbreviation, or did he use italics for more than one editorial purpose?
next_steps: Inspect ff. 108v–112v against the edition if a facsimile becomes available.
Consult any general editorial statement by Horstmann for this Englische Studien volume or related transcriptions.
Until verified, describe the XML HI elements as preserved print typography rather than as certain manuscript expansions.
